name: Pip names himself from his mispronunciation of Philip Pirrip
source_quote: My infant tongue could make of both names nothing longer or more explicit than Pip. So, I called myself Pip, and came to be called Pip.

name: Pip explains knowledge of his parents through his sister and tombstones
source_quote: As I never saw my father or my mother, and never saw any likeness of either of them, my first fancies regarding what they were like were unreasonably derived from their tombstones.

name: Pip forms childish imaginary portraits of his parents from inscriptions
source_quote: The shape of the letters on my father’s, gave me an odd idea that he was a square, stout, dark man, with curly black hair.

name: Pip imagines his dead brothers’ lives from their small gravestones
source_quote: I am indebted for a belief I religiously entertained that they had all been born on their backs with their hands in their trousers-pockets.

name: Pip situates his childhood in the marsh country and recalls a vivid afternoon
source_quote: Ours was the marsh country, down by the river, within, as the river wound, twenty miles of the sea.
time: a memorable raw afternoon towards evening
location: marsh country by the river, near the sea

name: Pip recognizes the graveyard, surrounding landscape, and his own identity
source_quote: I found out for certain that this bleak place overgrown with nettles was the churchyard... and that the small bundle of shivers... was Pip.
location: churchyard and adjacent marshes by the river and sea

name: A terrifying convict suddenly confronts Pip in the graveyard
source_quote: “Hold your noise!” cried a terrible voice, as a man started up from among the graves... “Keep still, you little devil, or I’ll cut your throat!”
time: immediately after Pip’s fearful recognition of the setting

name: Pip pleads for his life, revealing his terror before the convict
source_quote: “Oh! Don’t cut my throat, sir,” I pleaded in terror. “Pray don’t do it, sir.”

name: Convict forces Pip to reveal where he lives
source_quote: “Show us where you live,” said the man. “Pint out the place!”

name: Convict searches Pip and eats his bread
source_quote: turned me upside down, and emptied my pockets... while he ate the bread ravenously.
time: Immediately after Pip points out the village

name: Pip trembles while observing the man's roughness and hunger
source_quote: I was seated on a high tombstone, trembling while he ate the bread ravenously.
time: During and after the man's search and eating
location: On a high tombstone in the churchyard

name: Man comments on Pip’s physical appearance
source_quote: “You young dog,” said the man, licking his lips, “what fat cheeks you ha’ got.”

name: The man threatens to eat the child
source_quote: “Darn me if I couldn’t eat ’em,” said the man, with a threatening shake of his head...

name: Pip clings to the tombstone to steady himself and suppress tears
source_quote: I earnestly expressed my hope that he wouldn’t, and held tighter to the tombstone...

name: The man demands to know where Pip’s mother is
source_quote: “Now lookee here!” said the man. “Where’s your mother?”

name: Pip reveals his mother is buried in the churchyard
source_quote: “There, sir!” I timidly explained. “Also Georgiana. That’s my mother.”

name: Stranger questions Pip about his parents and guardians
source_quote: “Who d’ye live with,—supposin’ you’re kindly let to live, which I han’t made up my mind about?”
location: Graveyard (implicit from context of parents' graves)

name: Man reacts to learning Joe is a blacksmith and glances at his leg
source_quote: “Blacksmith, eh?” said he. And looked down at his leg.

name: Convict intimidates Pip and threatens his life while demanding help
source_quote: “You get me a file… And you get me wittles… Or I’ll have your heart and liver out.”

name: Pip pleads for physical relief so he can comply
source_quote: “If you would kindly please to let me keep upright, sir, perhaps I shouldn’t be sick, and perhaps I could attend more.”

name: Convict threatens Pip into bringing tools and food
source_quote: “You bring me, to-morrow morning early, that file and them wittles... and you shall be let to live.”

name: Pip promises to bring the file and food
source_quote: I said that I would get him the file, and I would get him what broken bits of food I could...
time: Immediately after the threat; for early next morning

name: The stranger warns Pip to remember his promise and sends him home
source_quote: “Now,” he pursued, “you remember what you’ve undertook, and you remember that young man, and you get home!”
time: immediately following Pip's encounter with the man

name: The man limps away across the graveyard toward the river, shivering and hugging himself
source_quote: He hugged his shuddering body in both his arms… and limped towards the low church wall.
location: from the churchyard towards the river across the marshes

name: Pip runs home while watching the man’s retreat and imagining the dead grasping at him
source_quote: He looked in my young eyes as if he were eluding the hands of the dead people, stretching up cautiously out of their graves, to get a twist upon his ankle and pull him in.
time: as the man climbs the wall and moves away
location: churchyard and view over the marshes

name: The landscape of marshes, river, and sky is described as stark and ominous
source_quote: The marshes were just a long black horizontal line then… and the sky was just a row of long angry red lines and dense black lines intermixed.
time: as Pip stops to look after the man

name: Pip observes a limping man moving toward a distant gibbet
source_quote: The man was limping on towards this latter, as if he were the pirate come to life, and come down, and going back to hook himself up again.
location: edge of the river near the beacon and gibbet

name: Pip imagines the limping man as a resurrected pirate and is terrified
source_quote: It gave me a terrible turn when I thought so; and as I saw the cattle lifting their heads to gaze after him, I wondered whether they thought so too.
time: immediately after observing the limping man

name: Pip, unable to see the ‘horrible young man,’ flees home in renewed fear
source_quote: I looked all round for the horrible young man, and could see no signs of him. But now I was frightened again, and ran home without stopping.
time: immediately after searching for the horrible young man
